lessons.lesson: Reel - 'Automation quietly killed my recurring contractor invoice' got 142 views and 1 like on YouTube Shorts (0.7% like rate). Modest launch performance, no strong positive signal — suggests hook or topic may need sharper entry point.

Money-saving automation case studies get moderate views but need stronger 'save-for-later' triggers. The hook 'I paid a contractor $1,200 a month' got attention but failed to convert viewers into savers — likely because the specific implementation was buried. Future 'cost-saving' reels should show the actual Zapier/automation setup visually to drive saves.
Portrait format (static image) consistently underperforms Reels by 3-10x on reach and views. Avoid portrait/static image format.

Posts with specific dollar amounts ($1,200, $1,400, $1,800) and personal 'I fired/payed' hooks significantly outperform generic contrarian or hot-take headlines on Instagram. Use personal cost-specific hooks.

Carousel and reel posts with hot-take topics about AI replacing jobs are getting very low engagement (11-46 reach, 0 saves, 5 likes). The 'specific number promise' hook format from earlier runs also underperformed. Need to pivot away from pure hot-take/negative-prediction angles toward more constructive, actionable content.

The '$40K pricing hallucination' topic underperformed severely on Instagram (reach=3, saves=0 vs 7.2 rolling avg). The 'regretful twist + specific dollar failure' angle resonated less than 'system builder' or 'cost saving success' narratives. Avoid 'AI failure/mistake post-mortems' as a primary angle — readers engage more with success architectures than cautionary tales.
